CMPSCI-4390 Deep Learning: Project Phase 4: Data Augmentation¶

Jen Putnam¶

In [ ]:
# Upload the image dataset from Google Drive
from google.colab import drive
drive.mount('/content/drive')

Mounted at /content/drive
In [ ]:
# Path of image dataset: pass to Image Data Generator
copied_path = "drive/MyDrive/landscapes"

Import Libraries¶

In [ ]:
from tensorflow import keras
from tensorflow.keras import layers
In [ ]:
import tensorflow as tf
In [ ]:
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
In [ ]:
from tensorflow.keras.preprocessing.image import ImageDataGenerator
In [ ]:
import PIL
print('Pillow Version:', PIL.__version__)
from PIL import Image

Pillow Version: 7.1.2
In [ ]:
from matplotlib.pyplot import imshow
import numpy as np
%matplotlib inline
In [ ]:
from keras.callbacks import EarlyStopping

First Experiment: Image Augmentation using ImageDataGenerator¶

In [ ]:
my_augmented_generator = ImageDataGenerator(
    width_shift_range=0.2,
    height_shift_range=0.2,
    shear_range=0.1,
    zoom_range=0.2,
    horizontal_flip=True,
    rescale=1./255,
    fill_mode='nearest')
In [ ]:
my_augmented_image_generator = my_augmented_generator.flow_from_directory(copied_path, target_size=(152, 152), batch_size=4, class_mode=None)

Found 1014 images belonging to 1 classes.
In [ ]:
for my_batch in my_augmented_image_generator:
    images = my_batch
    for i in range(len(images)):
        # Gives one image
        plt.imshow(images[i])
        plt.colorbar()
        plt.show()
    break

Build the Variational Autoencoder¶

In [ ]:
# Build encoder network, which turns a real image into two vectors (mean and variance) in the latent space
latent_dim = 2

encoder_inputs = keras.Input(shape=(152, 152, 3))
x = layers.Conv2D(32, 3, activation="relu", strides=2, padding="same")(encoder_inputs)
x = layers.Conv2D(32, 3, activation="relu", strides=1, padding="same")(x)
x = layers.Conv2D(64, 3, activation="relu", strides=2, padding="same")(x)
x = layers.Conv2D(64, 3, activation="relu", strides=1, padding="same")(x)
x = layers.Flatten()(x)
x = layers.Dense(16, activation="relu")(x)
z_mean = layers.Dense(latent_dim, name="z_mean")(x)
z_log_var = layers.Dense(latent_dim, name="z_log_var")(x)
encoder = keras.Model(encoder_inputs, [z_mean, z_log_var], name="encoder")
In [ ]:
# Show parameters
encoder.summary()

Model: "encoder"
__________________________________________________________________________________________________
 Layer (type)                   Output Shape         Param #     Connected to                     
==================================================================================================
 input_1 (InputLayer)           [(None, 152, 152, 3  0           []                               
                                )]                                                                
                                                                                                  
 conv2d (Conv2D)                (None, 76, 76, 32)   896         ['input_1[0][0]']                
                                                                                                  
 conv2d_1 (Conv2D)              (None, 76, 76, 32)   9248        ['conv2d[0][0]']                 
                                                                                                  
 conv2d_2 (Conv2D)              (None, 38, 38, 64)   18496       ['conv2d_1[0][0]']               
                                                                                                  
 conv2d_3 (Conv2D)              (None, 38, 38, 64)   36928       ['conv2d_2[0][0]']               
                                                                                                  
 flatten (Flatten)              (None, 92416)        0           ['conv2d_3[0][0]']               
                                                                                                  
 dense (Dense)                  (None, 16)           1478672     ['flatten[0][0]']                
                                                                                                  
 z_mean (Dense)                 (None, 2)            34          ['dense[0][0]']                  
                                                                                                  
 z_log_var (Dense)              (None, 2)            34          ['dense[0][0]']                  
                                                                                                  
==================================================================================================
Total params: 1,544,308
Trainable params: 1,544,308
Non-trainable params: 0
__________________________________________________________________________________________________
In [ ]:
# Use the mean and variance vectors, which are the parameters of the statistical distribution assumed to have
# produced the real input image, to generate a point in the latent space

class Sampler(layers.Layer):
    def call(self, z_mean, z_log_var):
        batch_size = tf.shape(z_mean)[0]
        z_size = tf.shape(z_mean)[1]
        epsilon = tf.random.normal(shape=(batch_size, z_size))
        return z_mean + tf.exp(0.5 * z_log_var) * epsilon
In [ ]:
# Build decoder network, which will take a point from the latent space and reconstruct it into an output
# image with the same dimensions as the input image

latent_inputs = keras.Input(shape=(latent_dim,))
x = layers.Dense(38 * 38 * 64, activation="relu")(latent_inputs)
x = layers.Reshape((38, 38, 64))(x)
x = layers.Conv2DTranspose(64, 3, activation="relu", strides=1, padding="same")(x)
x = layers.Conv2DTranspose(64, 3, activation="relu", strides=2, padding="same")(x)
x = layers.Conv2DTranspose(32, 3, activation="relu", strides=1, padding="same")(x)
x = layers.Conv2DTranspose(32, 3, activation="relu", strides=2, padding="same")(x)
decoder_outputs = layers.Conv2D(3, 3, activation="sigmoid", padding="same")(x)      # Changed number of filters to 3, so output tensor matches shape of inputs
decoder = keras.Model(latent_inputs, decoder_outputs, name="decoder")
In [ ]:
decoder.summary()

Model: "decoder"
_________________________________________________________________
 Layer (type)                Output Shape              Param #   
=================================================================
 input_3 (InputLayer)        [(None, 2)]               0         
                                                                 
 dense_2 (Dense)             (None, 92416)             277248    
                                                                 
 reshape_1 (Reshape)         (None, 38, 38, 64)        0         
                                                                 
 conv2d_transpose_4 (Conv2DT  (None, 38, 38, 64)       36928     
 ranspose)                                                       
                                                                 
 conv2d_transpose_5 (Conv2DT  (None, 76, 76, 64)       36928     
 ranspose)                                                       
                                                                 
 conv2d_transpose_6 (Conv2DT  (None, 76, 76, 32)       18464     
 ranspose)                                                       
                                                                 
 conv2d_transpose_7 (Conv2DT  (None, 152, 152, 32)     9248      
 ranspose)                                                       
                                                                 
 conv2d_5 (Conv2D)           (None, 152, 152, 3)       867       
                                                                 
=================================================================
Total params: 379,683
Trainable params: 379,683
Non-trainable params: 0
_________________________________________________________________
In [ ]:
# Build VAE model with custom training step for self-supervised learning

class VAE(keras.Model):
    def __init__(self, encoder, decoder, **kwargs):
        super().__init__(**kwargs)
        self.encoder = encoder
        self.decoder = decoder
        self.sampler = Sampler()
        self.total_loss_tracker = keras.metrics.Mean(name="total_loss")
        self.reconstruction_loss_tracker = keras.metrics.Mean(
            name="reconstruction_loss")
        self.kl_loss_tracker = keras.metrics.Mean(name="kl_loss")

    @property
    def metrics(self):
        return [self.total_loss_tracker,
                self.reconstruction_loss_tracker,
                self.kl_loss_tracker]

    def train_step(self, data):
        with tf.GradientTape() as tape:
            z_mean, z_log_var = self.encoder(data)
            z = self.sampler(z_mean, z_log_var)
            reconstruction = decoder(z)
            reconstruction_loss = tf.reduce_mean(
                tf.reduce_sum(
                    keras.losses.binary_crossentropy(data, reconstruction),       # This line throws an error if tensor returned by decoder()
                    axis=(1, 2)                                                   # does not match shape of input tensor
                )
            )
            kl_loss = -0.5 * (1 + z_log_var - tf.square(z_mean) - tf.exp(z_log_var))
            total_loss = reconstruction_loss + tf.reduce_mean(kl_loss)
        grads = tape.gradient(total_loss, self.trainable_weights)
        self.optimizer.apply_gradients(zip(grads, self.trainable_weights))
        self.total_loss_tracker.update_state(total_loss)
        self.reconstruction_loss_tracker.update_state(reconstruction_loss)
        self.kl_loss_tracker.update_state(kl_loss)
        return {
            "total_loss": self.total_loss_tracker.result(),
            "reconstruction_loss": self.reconstruction_loss_tracker.result(),
            "kl_loss": self.kl_loss_tracker.result(),
        }

Train the VAE and Monitor Total Loss and Regularization Loss¶

In [ ]:
# Compile the VAE
vae = VAE(encoder, decoder)
vae.compile(optimizer=keras.optimizers.Adam(), run_eagerly=True, metrics=['total_loss', 'reconstruction_loss', 'kl_loss'])
In [ ]:
# Load saved weights to continue training VAE
vae.load_weights('./weights/weights_1_4.tf')
Out[ ]:
<tensorflow.python.training.tracking.util.CheckpointLoadStatus at 0x7f03d095e2d0>
In [ ]:
# Train the VAE
callback_b = EarlyStopping(monitor='total_loss', mode='min', patience=25, verbose=1)
history = vae.fit(my_augmented_image_generator, epochs=200, batch_size=28, callbacks=[callback_b])

In [ ]:
# Check what's in the history
plt.figure(figsize=[9.5, 6.5])
plt.plot(history.history['total_loss'])
plt.title('Model loss') 
plt.ylabel('Loss') 
plt.xlabel('Epoch') 
plt.legend(['Total Loss'], loc='upper right') 
plt.show()
In [ ]:
plt.figure(figsize=[9.5, 6.5])
plt.plot(history.history['kl_loss'], color='orange')
plt.title('Model Regularization Loss') 
plt.ylabel('Loss') 
plt.xlabel('Epoch') 
plt.legend(['Regularization Loss'], loc='upper right') 
plt.show()
In [ ]:
# Save weights of VAE
vae.save_weights('./weights/weights_1_4.tf', save_format='tf')

Visualize Images Sampled from the Learned Latent Space¶

In [ ]:
def display_image_sequence(x_start,y_start,x_end,y_end,no_of_imgs):
    x_axis = np.linspace(x_start,x_end,no_of_imgs)
    y_axis = np.linspace(y_start,y_end,no_of_imgs)
    
    x_axis = x_axis[:, np.newaxis]
    y_axis = y_axis[:, np.newaxis]
    
    new_points = np.hstack((x_axis, y_axis))
    new_images = decoder.predict(new_points)
    new_images = new_images.reshape(new_images.shape[0], new_images.shape[1], new_images.shape[2], new_images.shape[3])
    
    # Display some images
    fig, axes = plt.subplots(ncols=no_of_imgs, sharex=False,
                             sharey=True, figsize=(20, 7))
    counter = 0
    for i in range(no_of_imgs):
        axes[counter].imshow(new_images[i])
        axes[counter].get_xaxis().set_visible(False)
        axes[counter].get_yaxis().set_visible(False)
        counter += 1
    plt.show()

display_image_sequence(0,2,2,0,9)

Mapping Input to Output¶

# Load weights before calling predict
vae.load_weights('./weights/weights_1_4.tf')

# Show Latent Representation
example = np.array([img])
z_mean, z_log_var = vae.encoder.predict(example)
sampler = Sampler()
z = sampler(z_mean, z_log_var)
print(z_mean)
print(z_log_var)
print(z)

In [ ]:
# Show Decoded Output
reconstruction = vae.decoder.predict(z)
reconstructed_img = reconstruction.reshape(152, 152, 3)
imshow(np.asarray(reconstructed_img))
plt.imshow(np.asarray(reconstructed_img))
plt.colorbar()
plt.show()

Second Experiment: Try Less Augmentation¶

In [ ]:
from tensorflow.keras.preprocessing.image import ImageDataGenerator
In [ ]:
my_augmented_generator_2 = ImageDataGenerator(
    width_shift_range=0.1,
    height_shift_range=0.1,
    shear_range=0.05,
    zoom_range=0.1,
    horizontal_flip=True,
    rescale=1./255,
    fill_mode='reflect')
In [ ]:
my_augmented_image_generator_2 = my_augmented_generator_2.flow_from_directory(copied_path, target_size=(152, 152), batch_size=4, class_mode=None)

Found 1014 images belonging to 1 classes.
In [ ]:
for my_batch_2 in my_augmented_image_generator_2:
    images_2 = my_batch_2
    for i in range(len(images_2)):
        # Gives one image
        plt.imshow(images_2[i])
        plt.colorbar()
        plt.show()
    break
In [ ]:

Train the VAE and Monitor Total Loss and Regularization Loss¶

In [ ]:
callback_b = EarlyStopping(monitor='total_loss', mode='min', patience=25, verbose=1)
In [ ]:
# Train the VAE
history = vae.fit(my_augmented_image_generator_2, epochs=200, batch_size=28, callbacks=[callback_b])

plt.figure(figsize=[9.5, 6.5])
plt.plot(history.history['total_loss'])
plt.title('Model loss') 
plt.ylabel('Loss') 
plt.xlabel('Epoch') 
plt.legend(['Total Loss'], loc='upper right') 
plt.show()

plt.figure(figsize=[9.5, 6.5])
plt.plot(history.history['kl_loss'], color='orange')
plt.title('Model Regularization Loss') 
plt.ylabel('Loss') 
plt.xlabel('Epoch') 
plt.legend(['Regularization Loss'], loc='upper right') 
plt.show()

Visualize Images Sampled from Learned Latent Space¶

In [ ]:
def display_image_sequence(x_start,y_start,x_end,y_end,no_of_imgs):
    x_axis = np.linspace(x_start,x_end,no_of_imgs)
    y_axis = np.linspace(y_start,y_end,no_of_imgs)
    
    x_axis = x_axis[:, np.newaxis]
    y_axis = y_axis[:, np.newaxis]
    
    new_points = np.hstack((x_axis, y_axis))
    new_images = decoder.predict(new_points)
    new_images = new_images.reshape(new_images.shape[0], new_images.shape[1], new_images.shape[2], new_images.shape[3])
    
    # Display some images
    fig, axes = plt.subplots(ncols=no_of_imgs, sharex=False,
                             sharey=True, figsize=(20, 7))
    counter = 0
    for i in range(no_of_imgs):
        axes[counter].imshow(new_images[i])
        axes[counter].get_xaxis().set_visible(False)
        axes[counter].get_yaxis().set_visible(False)
        counter += 1
    plt.show()

display_image_sequence(0,2,2,0,9)

Mapping Input to Output¶

sampler = Sampler()
z = sampler(z_mean, z_log_var)
print(z_mean)
print(z_log_var)

reconstructed_img = reconstruction.reshape(152, 152, 3)
imshow(np.asarray(reconstructed_img))

Third Experiment: Try More Augmentation¶

In [ ]:
my_augmented_generator_3 = ImageDataGenerator(
    width_shift_range=0.3,
    height_shift_range=0.3,
    shear_range=0.15,
    zoom_range=0.3,
    horizontal_flip=True,
    rescale=1./255,
    fill_mode='reflect')
In [ ]:
my_augmented_image_generator_3 = my_augmented_generator_3.flow_from_directory(copied_path, target_size=(152, 152), batch_size=4, class_mode=None)

Found 1014 images belonging to 1 classes.
In [ ]:
for my_batch_3 in my_augmented_image_generator_3:
    images_3 = my_batch_3
    for i in range(len(images_3)):
        # Gives one image
        plt.imshow(images_3[i])
        plt.colorbar()
        plt.show()
    break

Train the VAE and Monitor Total Loss and Regularization Loss¶

In [ ]:
# Compile the VAE
vae = VAE(encoder, decoder)
vae.compile(optimizer=keras.optimizers.Adam(), run_eagerly=True, metrics=['total_loss', 'reconstruction_loss', 'kl_loss'])
In [ ]:
# Train the VAE
history = vae.fit(my_augmented_image_generator_3, epochs=200, batch_size=28, callbacks=[callback_b])

Visualize Images Sampled from Learned Latent Space¶

In [ ]:
def display_image_sequence(x_start,y_start,x_end,y_end,no_of_imgs):
    x_axis = np.linspace(x_start,x_end,no_of_imgs)
    y_axis = np.linspace(y_start,y_end,no_of_imgs)
    
    x_axis = x_axis[:, np.newaxis]
    y_axis = y_axis[:, np.newaxis]
    
    new_points = np.hstack((x_axis, y_axis))
    new_images = decoder.predict(new_points)
    new_images = new_images.reshape(new_images.shape[0], new_images.shape[1], new_images.shape[2], new_images.shape[3])
    
    # Display some images
    fig, axes = plt.subplots(ncols=no_of_imgs, sharex=False,
                             sharey=True, figsize=(20, 7))
    counter = 0
    for i in range(no_of_imgs):
        axes[counter].imshow(new_images[i])
        axes[counter].get_xaxis().set_visible(False)
        axes[counter].get_yaxis().set_visible(False)
        counter += 1
    plt.show()

display_image_sequence(0,2,2,0,9)

print(images_gray[0].shape)

(152, 152, 1)

Build the Variational Autoencoder¶

In [ ]:
# Build encoder network, which turns a real image into two vectors (mean and variance) in the latent space
latent_dim = 2

encoder_inputs = keras.Input(shape=(152, 152, 1))
x = layers.Conv2D(32, 3, activation="relu", strides=2, padding="same")(encoder_inputs)
x = layers.Conv2D(32, 3, activation="relu", strides=1, padding="same")(x)
x = layers.Conv2D(64, 3, activation="relu", strides=2, padding="same")(x)
x = layers.Conv2D(64, 3, activation="relu", strides=1, padding="same")(x)
x = layers.Flatten()(x)
x = layers.Dense(16, activation="relu")(x)
z_mean = layers.Dense(latent_dim, name="z_mean")(x)
z_log_var = layers.Dense(latent_dim, name="z_log_var")(x)
encoder = keras.Model(encoder_inputs, [z_mean, z_log_var], name="encoder")
In [ ]:
# Show parameters
encoder.summary()

Model: "encoder"
__________________________________________________________________________________________________
 Layer (type)                   Output Shape         Param #     Connected to                     
==================================================================================================
 input_1 (InputLayer)           [(None, 152, 152, 1  0           []                               
                                )]                                                                
                                                                                                  
 conv2d (Conv2D)                (None, 76, 76, 32)   320         ['input_1[0][0]']                
                                                                                                  
 conv2d_1 (Conv2D)              (None, 76, 76, 32)   9248        ['conv2d[0][0]']                 
                                                                                                  
 conv2d_2 (Conv2D)              (None, 38, 38, 64)   18496       ['conv2d_1[0][0]']               
                                                                                                  
 conv2d_3 (Conv2D)              (None, 38, 38, 64)   36928       ['conv2d_2[0][0]']               
                                                                                                  
 flatten (Flatten)              (None, 92416)        0           ['conv2d_3[0][0]']               
                                                                                                  
 dense (Dense)                  (None, 16)           1478672     ['flatten[0][0]']                
                                                                                                  
 z_mean (Dense)                 (None, 2)            34          ['dense[0][0]']                  
                                                                                                  
 z_log_var (Dense)              (None, 2)            34          ['dense[0][0]']                  
                                                                                                  
==================================================================================================
Total params: 1,543,732
Trainable params: 1,543,732
Non-trainable params: 0
__________________________________________________________________________________________________
In [ ]:
# Use the mean and variance vectors, which are the parameters of the statistical distribution assumed to have
# produced the real input image, to generate a point in the latent space

class Sampler(layers.Layer):
    def call(self, z_mean, z_log_var):
        batch_size = tf.shape(z_mean)[0]
        z_size = tf.shape(z_mean)[1]
        epsilon = tf.random.normal(shape=(batch_size, z_size))
        return z_mean + tf.exp(0.5 * z_log_var) * epsilon
In [ ]:
# Build decoder network, which will take a point from the latent space and reconstruct it into an output
# image with the same dimensions as the input image

latent_inputs = keras.Input(shape=(latent_dim,))
x = layers.Dense(38 * 38 * 64, activation="relu")(latent_inputs)
x = layers.Reshape((38, 38, 64))(x)
x = layers.Conv2DTranspose(64, 3, activation="relu", strides=1, padding="same")(x)
x = layers.Conv2DTranspose(64, 3, activation="relu", strides=2, padding="same")(x)
x = layers.Conv2DTranspose(32, 3, activation="relu", strides=1, padding="same")(x)
x = layers.Conv2DTranspose(32, 3, activation="relu", strides=2, padding="same")(x)
decoder_outputs = layers.Conv2D(1, 3, activation="sigmoid", padding="same")(x)      # Changed number of filters to 3, so output tensor matches shape of inputs
decoder = keras.Model(latent_inputs, decoder_outputs, name="decoder")
In [ ]:
# Show parameters
decoder.summary()

Model: "decoder"
_________________________________________________________________
 Layer (type)                Output Shape              Param #   
=================================================================
 input_2 (InputLayer)        [(None, 2)]               0         
                                                                 
 dense_1 (Dense)             (None, 92416)             277248    
                                                                 
 reshape (Reshape)           (None, 38, 38, 64)        0         
                                                                 
 conv2d_transpose (Conv2DTra  (None, 38, 38, 64)       36928     
 nspose)                                                         
                                                                 
 conv2d_transpose_1 (Conv2DT  (None, 76, 76, 64)       36928     
 ranspose)                                                       
                                                                 
 conv2d_transpose_2 (Conv2DT  (None, 76, 76, 32)       18464     
 ranspose)                                                       
                                                                 
 conv2d_transpose_3 (Conv2DT  (None, 152, 152, 32)     9248      
 ranspose)                                                       
                                                                 
 conv2d_4 (Conv2D)           (None, 152, 152, 1)       289       
                                                                 
=================================================================
Total params: 379,105
Trainable params: 379,105
Non-trainable params: 0
_________________________________________________________________
In [ ]:
# Build VAE model with custom training step for self-supervised learning

class VAE(keras.Model):
    def __init__(self, encoder, decoder, **kwargs):
        super().__init__(**kwargs)
        self.encoder = encoder
        self.decoder = decoder
        self.sampler = Sampler()
        self.total_loss_tracker = keras.metrics.Mean(name="total_loss")
        self.reconstruction_loss_tracker = keras.metrics.Mean(
            name="reconstruction_loss")
        self.kl_loss_tracker = keras.metrics.Mean(name="kl_loss")

    @property
    def metrics(self):
        return [self.total_loss_tracker,
                self.reconstruction_loss_tracker,
                self.kl_loss_tracker]

    def train_step(self, data):
        with tf.GradientTape() as tape:
            z_mean, z_log_var = self.encoder(data)
            z = self.sampler(z_mean, z_log_var)
            reconstruction = decoder(z)
            reconstruction_loss = tf.reduce_mean(
                tf.reduce_sum(
                    keras.losses.binary_crossentropy(data, reconstruction),       # This line throws an error if tensor returned by decoder()
                    axis=(1, 2)                                                   # does not match shape of input tensor
                )
            )
            kl_loss = -0.5 * (1 + z_log_var - tf.square(z_mean) - tf.exp(z_log_var))
            total_loss = reconstruction_loss + tf.reduce_mean(kl_loss)
        grads = tape.gradient(total_loss, self.trainable_weights)
        self.optimizer.apply_gradients(zip(grads, self.trainable_weights))
        self.total_loss_tracker.update_state(total_loss)
        self.reconstruction_loss_tracker.update_state(reconstruction_loss)
        self.kl_loss_tracker.update_state(kl_loss)
        return {
            "total_loss": self.total_loss_tracker.result(),
            "reconstruction_loss": self.reconstruction_loss_tracker.result(),
            "kl_loss": self.kl_loss_tracker.result(),
        }

Train the VAE and Monitor Total Loss and Regularization Loss¶

In [ ]:
# Compile the VAE
vae = VAE(encoder, decoder)
vae.compile(optimizer=keras.optimizers.Adam(), run_eagerly=True, metrics=['total_loss', 'reconstruction_loss', 'kl_loss'])
In [ ]:
# Train the VAE
callback_b = EarlyStopping(monitor='total_loss', mode='min', patience=25, verbose=1)
history = vae.fit(my_augmented_image_generator_grayscale, epochs=200, batch_size=28, callbacks=[callback_b])

In [ ]:
# Check what's in the history
plt.figure(figsize=[9.5, 6.5])
plt.plot(history.history['total_loss'])
plt.title('Model loss') 
plt.ylabel('Loss') 
plt.xlabel('Epoch') 
plt.legend(['Total Loss'], loc='upper right') 
plt.show()
In [ ]:
plt.figure(figsize=[9.5, 6.5])
plt.plot(history.history['kl_loss'], color='orange')
plt.title('Model Regularization Loss') 
plt.ylabel('Loss') 
plt.xlabel('Epoch') 
plt.legend(['Regularization Loss'], loc='upper right') 
plt.show()
In [ ]:
# Save weights
vae.save_weights('./weights/weights_1_gray.tf', save_format='tf')

Visualize Images Sampled from Learned Latent Space¶

In [ ]:
def display_image_sequence(x_start,y_start,x_end,y_end,no_of_imgs):
    x_axis = np.linspace(x_start,x_end,no_of_imgs)
    y_axis = np.linspace(y_start,y_end,no_of_imgs)
    
    x_axis = x_axis[:, np.newaxis]
    y_axis = y_axis[:, np.newaxis]
    
    new_points = np.hstack((x_axis, y_axis))
    new_images = decoder.predict(new_points)
    new_images = new_images.reshape(new_images.shape[0], new_images.shape[1], new_images.shape[2], new_images.shape[3])
    
    # Display some images
    fig, axes = plt.subplots(ncols=no_of_imgs, sharex=False,
                             sharey=True, figsize=(20, 7))
    counter = 0
    for i in range(no_of_imgs):
        gray_img = np.reshape(new_images[i], (152,152))
        axes[counter].imshow(gray_img, cmap='gray')
        axes[counter].get_xaxis().set_visible(False)
        axes[counter].get_yaxis().set_visible(False)
        counter += 1
    plt.show()

display_image_sequence(0,2,2,0,9)

Mapping Input to Output¶

In [ ]:
# Convert to grayscale
image = Image.open('/content/drive/MyDrive/landscapes/landscapeImg/download (37).jpg')

img_resized =gs_image.resize((152,152))
imshow(np.asarray(img_resized), cmap='gray')

# Show Latent Representation
example = np.array([gs_image])
z_mean, z_log_var = vae.encoder.predict(example)
sampler = Sampler()
z = sampler(z_mean, z_log_var)
print(z_mean)
print(z_log_var)
print(z)

# Show Decoded Output
reconstruction = vae.decoder.predict(z)
reconstructed_img = reconstruction.reshape(152, 152, 1)
reconstructed_img = np.reshape(reconstructed_img, (152,152))
imshow(np.asarray(reconstructed_img))
plt.imshow(np.asarray(reconstructed_img), cmap='gray')
plt.colorbar()
plt.show()

    rescale=1./255)

Found 1014 images belonging to 1 classes.
In [ ]:
for my_batch in my_augmented_image_generator_rsz:
    images = my_batch
    for i in range(len(images)):
        # Gives one image
        plt.imshow(images[i])
        plt.colorbar()
        plt.show()
    break

Build the Variational Autoencoder¶

In [ ]:
# Build encoder network, which turns a real image into two vectors (mean and variance) in the latent space
latent_dim = 2

encoder_inputs = keras.Input(shape=(28, 28, 3))
x = layers.Conv2D(32, 3, activation="relu", strides=2, padding="same")(encoder_inputs)
x = layers.Conv2D(32, 3, activation="relu", strides=1, padding="same")(x)
x = layers.Conv2D(64, 3, activation="relu", strides=2, padding="same")(x)
x = layers.Conv2D(64, 3, activation="relu", strides=1, padding="same")(x)
x = layers.Flatten()(x)
x = layers.Dense(16, activation="relu")(x)
z_mean = layers.Dense(latent_dim, name="z_mean")(x)
z_log_var = layers.Dense(latent_dim, name="z_log_var")(x)
encoder = keras.Model(encoder_inputs, [z_mean, z_log_var], name="encoder")
In [ ]:
# Show parameters
encoder.summary()

Model: "encoder"
__________________________________________________________________________________________________
 Layer (type)                   Output Shape         Param #     Connected to                     
==================================================================================================
 input_1 (InputLayer)           [(None, 28, 28, 3)]  0           []                               
                                                                                                  
 conv2d (Conv2D)                (None, 14, 14, 32)   896         ['input_1[0][0]']                
                                                                                                  
 conv2d_1 (Conv2D)              (None, 14, 14, 32)   9248        ['conv2d[0][0]']                 
                                                                                                  
 conv2d_2 (Conv2D)              (None, 7, 7, 64)     18496       ['conv2d_1[0][0]']               
                                                                                                  
 conv2d_3 (Conv2D)              (None, 7, 7, 64)     36928       ['conv2d_2[0][0]']               
                                                                                                  
 flatten (Flatten)              (None, 3136)         0           ['conv2d_3[0][0]']               
                                                                                                  
 dense (Dense)                  (None, 16)           50192       ['flatten[0][0]']                
                                                                                                  
 z_mean (Dense)                 (None, 2)            34          ['dense[0][0]']                  
                                                                                                  
 z_log_var (Dense)              (None, 2)            34          ['dense[0][0]']                  
                                                                                                  
==================================================================================================
Total params: 115,828
Trainable params: 115,828
Non-trainable params: 0
__________________________________________________________________________________________________
In [ ]:
# Use the mean and variance vectors, which are the parameters of the statistical distribution assumed to have
# produced the real input image, to generate a point in the latent space

class Sampler(layers.Layer):
    def call(self, z_mean, z_log_var):
        batch_size = tf.shape(z_mean)[0]
        z_size = tf.shape(z_mean)[1]
        epsilon = tf.random.normal(shape=(batch_size, z_size))
        return z_mean + tf.exp(0.5 * z_log_var) * epsilon
In [ ]:
# Build decoder network, which will take a point from the latent space and reconstruct it into an output
# image with the same dimensions as the input image

latent_inputs = keras.Input(shape=(latent_dim,))
x = layers.Dense(7 * 7 * 64, activation="relu")(latent_inputs)
x = layers.Reshape((7, 7, 64))(x)
x = layers.Conv2DTranspose(64, 3, activation="relu", strides=1, padding="same")(x)
x = layers.Conv2DTranspose(64, 3, activation="relu", strides=2, padding="same")(x)
x = layers.Conv2DTranspose(32, 3, activation="relu", strides=1, padding="same")(x)
x = layers.Conv2DTranspose(32, 3, activation="relu", strides=2, padding="same")(x)
decoder_outputs = layers.Conv2D(3, 3, activation="sigmoid", padding="same")(x)      # Changed number of filters to 3, so output tensor matches shape of inputs
decoder = keras.Model(latent_inputs, decoder_outputs, name="decoder")
In [ ]:
decoder.summary()

Model: "decoder"
_________________________________________________________________
 Layer (type)                Output Shape              Param #   
=================================================================
 input_2 (InputLayer)        [(None, 2)]               0         
                                                                 
 dense_1 (Dense)             (None, 3136)              9408      
                                                                 
 reshape (Reshape)           (None, 7, 7, 64)          0         
                                                                 
 conv2d_transpose (Conv2DTra  (None, 7, 7, 64)         36928     
 nspose)                                                         
                                                                 
 conv2d_transpose_1 (Conv2DT  (None, 14, 14, 64)       36928     
 ranspose)                                                       
                                                                 
 conv2d_transpose_2 (Conv2DT  (None, 14, 14, 32)       18464     
 ranspose)                                                       
                                                                 
 conv2d_transpose_3 (Conv2DT  (None, 28, 28, 32)       9248      
 ranspose)                                                       
                                                                 
 conv2d_4 (Conv2D)           (None, 28, 28, 3)         867       
                                                                 
=================================================================
Total params: 111,843
Trainable params: 111,843
Non-trainable params: 0
_________________________________________________________________
In [ ]:
# Build VAE model with custom training step for self-supervised learning

class VAE(keras.Model):
    def __init__(self, encoder, decoder, **kwargs):
        super().__init__(**kwargs)
        self.encoder = encoder
        self.decoder = decoder
        self.sampler = Sampler()
        self.total_loss_tracker = keras.metrics.Mean(name="total_loss")
        self.reconstruction_loss_tracker = keras.metrics.Mean(
            name="reconstruction_loss")
        self.kl_loss_tracker = keras.metrics.Mean(name="kl_loss")

    @property
    def metrics(self):
        return [self.total_loss_tracker,
                self.reconstruction_loss_tracker,
                self.kl_loss_tracker]

    def train_step(self, data):
        with tf.GradientTape() as tape:
            z_mean, z_log_var = self.encoder(data)
            z = self.sampler(z_mean, z_log_var)
            reconstruction = decoder(z)
            reconstruction_loss = tf.reduce_mean(
                tf.reduce_sum(
                    keras.losses.binary_crossentropy(data, reconstruction),       # This line throws an error if tensor returned by decoder()
                    axis=(1, 2)                                                   # does not match shape of input tensor
                )
            )
            kl_loss = -0.5 * (1 + z_log_var - tf.square(z_mean) - tf.exp(z_log_var))
            total_loss = reconstruction_loss + tf.reduce_mean(kl_loss)
        grads = tape.gradient(total_loss, self.trainable_weights)
        self.optimizer.apply_gradients(zip(grads, self.trainable_weights))
        self.total_loss_tracker.update_state(total_loss)
        self.reconstruction_loss_tracker.update_state(reconstruction_loss)
        self.kl_loss_tracker.update_state(kl_loss)
        return {
            "total_loss": self.total_loss_tracker.result(),
            "reconstruction_loss": self.reconstruction_loss_tracker.result(),
            "kl_loss": self.kl_loss_tracker.result(),
        }

Train the VAE and Monitor Total Loss and Regularization Loss¶

In [ ]:
# Compile the VAE
vae = VAE(encoder, decoder)
vae.compile(optimizer=keras.optimizers.Adam(), run_eagerly=True, metrics=['total_loss', 'reconstruction_loss', 'kl_loss'])
In [ ]:
# Train the VAE
callback_b = EarlyStopping(monitor='total_loss', mode='min', patience=25, verbose=1)
history = vae.fit(my_augmented_image_generator_rsz, epochs=200, batch_size=28, callbacks=[callback_b])

In [ ]:
# Save weights of VAE
vae.save_weights('./weights/weights_1_rsz.tf', save_format='tf')
In [ ]:
# Check what's in the history
plt.figure(figsize=[9.5, 6.5])
plt.plot(history.history['total_loss'])
plt.title('Model loss') 
plt.ylabel('Loss') 
plt.xlabel('Epoch') 
plt.legend(['Total Loss'], loc='upper right') 
plt.show()
In [ ]:
plt.figure(figsize=[9.5, 6.5])
plt.plot(history.history['kl_loss'], color='orange')
plt.title('Model Regularization Loss') 
plt.ylabel('Loss') 
plt.xlabel('Epoch') 
plt.legend(['Regularization Loss'], loc='upper right') 
plt.show()

Visualize Images Sampled from Learned Latent Space¶

In [ ]:
def display_image_sequence(x_start,y_start,x_end,y_end,no_of_imgs):
    x_axis = np.linspace(x_start,x_end,no_of_imgs)
    y_axis = np.linspace(y_start,y_end,no_of_imgs)
    
    x_axis = x_axis[:, np.newaxis]
    y_axis = y_axis[:, np.newaxis]
    
    new_points = np.hstack((x_axis, y_axis))
    new_images = decoder.predict(new_points)
    new_images = new_images.reshape(new_images.shape[0], new_images.shape[1], new_images.shape[2], new_images.shape[3])
    
    # Display some images
    fig, axes = plt.subplots(ncols=no_of_imgs, sharex=False,
                             sharey=True, figsize=(20, 7))
    counter = 0
    for i in range(no_of_imgs):
        axes[counter].imshow(new_images[i])
        axes[counter].get_xaxis().set_visible(False)
        axes[counter].get_yaxis().set_visible(False)
        counter += 1
    plt.show()

display_image_sequence(0,2,2,0,9)

Mapping Input to Output¶

In [ ]:
# Show input image
input_img = Image.open('/content/drive/MyDrive/landscapes/landscapeImg/download (37).jpg')
img_resized = input_img.resize((28, 28))
img = np.asarray(img_resized)
img.reshape(28, 28, 3)
print(img.shape)
imshow(np.asarray(img))
plt.imshow(np.asarray(img))
plt.colorbar()

(28, 28, 3)
Out[ ]:
<matplotlib.colorbar.Colorbar at 0x7fef50f74b50>
In [ ]:
# Show Latent Representation
example = np.array([img])
z_mean, z_log_var = vae.encoder.predict(example)
sampler = Sampler()
z = sampler(z_mean, z_log_var)
print(z_mean)
print(z_log_var)
print(z)

[[0.00152161 0.00041887]]
[[-0.00581504 -0.01090612]]
tf.Tensor([[ 0.42198744 -0.7928735 ]], shape=(1, 2), dtype=float32)
In [ ]:
# Show Decoded Output
reconstruction = vae.decoder.predict(z)
reconstructed_img = reconstruction.reshape(28, 28, 3)
imshow(np.asarray(reconstructed_img))
plt.imshow(np.asarray(reconstructed_img))
plt.colorbar()
plt.show()
